key_strengths: $25.2B ARR with 96%+ subscription mix and high switching costs embedded in professional workflows
41% FCF margin and ~33% ROIC — top-tier capital efficiency in global software
89% gross margins stable across five consecutive years, demonstrating durable pricing power
Near-net-cash balance sheet with 33x interest coverage; effectively impregnable
AI-first ARR tripling YoY validates Firefly as a credible new monetization driver
Trading at less than half 5-year median multiples (15x forward P/E vs. 33x historical)
key_risks: Canva's 260M MAUs and AI-native tools structurally eroding the lower end of the creative market
CEO departure with no named successor — leadership vacuum at critical AI strategy pivot
Thin price margin of safety: bear-case DCF of ~$160–$180 is only 28–36% below current ~$249
Firefly monetization opacity — AI-first ARR absolute dollar scale not yet publicly disclosed
Segment consolidation in FY2026 reduces analyst visibility into Digital Media vs. Experience Cloud performance
red_flags: CEO Narayen departure announced same day as Q1 FY2026 earnings with no successor named
Goldman Sachs Sell rating at $290 — sophisticated institutional money still sees downside from here
SBC at 8.2% of revenue elevated for a mature software company
Failed $20B Figma acquisition attempt signals occasional M&A discipline lapses
Segment consolidation removes external performance visibility at the worst possible time
buy_triggers: High-credibility CEO successor named with clear AI strategy mandate, removing leadership discount
AI-first ARR reaches disclosed scale of $2B+ or Creative Cloud ARR growth re-accelerates above 12%
Price declines to $200 or below, compressing bear-case downside to ~15–20%
sell_triggers: Creative Cloud ARR growth falls below 5% for two consecutive quarters with rising disclosed churn
CEO successor of low quality or poor strategic fit, or key product/AI leadership departs post-transition
Price reaches $450+ without fundamental AI metric improvement — take partial profits and reassess
valuation_summary: At ~$249 ADBE trades at 15x forward earnings and 11x P/FCF — less than half its 5-year median multiple — with third-party DCF models converging on intrinsic value of $313–$434 (base case ~$390); the market is pricing near-stagnation, which requires zero benefit from 10.9% ARR growth, and meaningful margin of safety exists only if AI disruption remains manageable.
source_summary: Analysis draws on Adobe Q1 FY2026 earnings release (March 12, 2026), FY2025 annual financials, SEC 10-Q filings, third-party DCF models (GuruFocus, Alpha Spread, valueinvesting.io), analyst consensus data, and web search results through March 15, 2026; all figures are current as of report date.
